properties.remark: GREEN COUNTY DISPATCH REPORTED ONGOING WATER RESCUE NEAR KY-1913 AND COBURG ROAD. A VEHICLE IS LOCATED IN HIGH WATER, WITH THREE PEOPLE ON TOP OF VEHICLE. MULTIPLE DEPARTMENTS ON SCENE ATTEMPTING WATER RESCUE. DISPATCH RECEIVED FIRST CALL FOR WATER RESCUE AROUND 5:18 AM EASTERN TIME.

GREEN COUNTY DISPATCH REPORTED A SECOND VEHICLE DROVE INTO HIGH WATER NEAR KY-1913 AND COBURG ROAD. 1 PERSON WAS RESCUED DOWN STREAM AFTER HOLDING ON TO A TREE IN SWIFT WATER. INJURIES ARE UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME. THIS WATER RESCUE IS SEPARATE FROM THE ONGOING WATER RESCUE, BUT IS IN THE SAME AREA.
DELAYED REPORT. TAYLOR COUNTY DISPATCH REPORTED TWO VEHICLES DROVE INTO HIGH WATER ON NORTH COBURG RD.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH 80 MPH WINDS IMPACTED PORTIONS OF HART COUNTY ON JANUARY 1, 2022. THE TORNADO WAS 0.4 MILES IN LENGTH, WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 1 MINUTE, AND WAS 50 YARDS IN MAXIMUM WIDTH. PLEASE SEE THE ASSOCIATED PNS FOR MORE DETAILS ABOUT THIS TORNADO.

DELAYED REPORT KENTUCKY MESONET STATION NEAR LORETTO. GUSTS MEASURED IN 5-MINUTE INCREMENTS AND TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON THAT AND RADAR OBSERVATIONS. THERE WAS SOME DAMAGE TO NEARBY BARNS ON SPENCER-HAMILTON RD.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN EAST OF OLMSTEAD AND TRACKED FOR 11.03 MILES TO 6 MILES SOUTHEAST OF RUSSELLVILLE, KY. ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS WERE 110 MPH WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. PLEASE SEE ASSOCIATED PNS FOR MORE INFORMATION.

SIMPSON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGMENT REPORTED DAMAGE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF MORGANTOWN ROAD AND WINSTON LANE. SOME TREES WERE UPROOTED OR LARGE BRANCHES SNAPPED. SHINGLE AND ANTENNA DAMAGE OCCURRED TO SOME HOMES. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 65 TO 70 MPH.

*** 1 FATAL *** CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLASH FLOOD REPORT FROM 4 NE MANVILLE. JEFFERSON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS HOMES WASHED AWAY ALONG EAST BRUSHY FORK ROAD. THE BODY OF AN ELDERLY WOMAN WAS FOUND 5 MILES DOWNSTREAM FROM WHERE HER HOME WAS SWEPT AWAY.

FLOODING ON N MAIN ST. IN FRANKLIN. THE ROAD HAD TO BE CLOSED. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
CONNECTOR ROAD HAD TO BE CLOSED DOWN DUE TO FLASH FLOODING ALONG THE LITTLE KENTUCKY RIVER AND HARDY CREEK. 5 OTHER ROADS IN THE COUNTY HAD MINOR FLOODING.

A HOME WAS SWEPT OFF OF ITS FOUNDATION AT 7701 HATTON ROAD, ALONG THE DUTCH FORK. TWO PEOPLE HAD TO BE RESCUED FROM THAT HOME. 7 OTHER HOMES WERE DAMAGED DOWN THE DUTCH FORK ALONG QUISENBERRY LANE. TIME COLLABORATED FROM EM AND RADAR.

PORTION OF STOCKTON ROAD WASHED AWAY IMMEDIATELY SOUTH OF THE NORTH BENSON CREEK BRIDGE. ROADWAY BLACKTOP WASHED AWAY AND ACCESS TO THE BRIDGE IS CUT OFF UNTIL REPAIRS ARE MADE TO THE ROAD. A PICKUP TRUCK WAS ALSO PINNED AGAINST A TREE BUT THE DRIVER WAS ABLE TO MAKE IT OUT WITHOUT INJURY.

DUBOIS COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED THAT FIRE DEPARTMENT ASSISTANCE WAS BEING REQUESTED TO PUMP WATER OUT OF THE SCHOOL BASEMENT IN THE TOWN OF DUBOIS. ALSO A POND OVERFLOWED THAT RARELY HAS ISSUES.
HIGHWAY 527 IN NORTHERN TAYLOR COUNTY WAS IMPASSABLE DUE TO HIGH WATER OVER THE ROADWAY.
